Dead-path retired script: not pytest-collected, not on any live import path, so collection+oracle unaffected.","scripts/_retired_2026_06_14/pg_compose_production_scale.py:45 same broken import `from archive._2026_06_14_retired_scripts.pg_compose_openai_validation import OpenAIShimClient` -> ModuleNotFoundError (same dead-path retired script)."]} (oracle byte-identical 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98, collection at baseline, 2 dangling refs).
Ask codex: "Are all executed file renames reference-complete (every importer fixed, no dangling reference, no dynamic/string/CLI/CI reference to a renamed file left broken)? Is the launcher consolidation behaviour-preserving (old scripts still work as call-throughs, no behavioural drift)? Were the DYNAMIC-REF files correctly skipped? End with FILE-RENAME-SAFE or FILE-RENAME-REVISE (+ the specific broken reference)."
Return verdict + points.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll gate this with CODEX. Let me first verify the key claims in the evidence before writing the gate file, then run CODEX.

content.content:
=== v24 (one of the 'consolidated' scripts) content ===
31 scripts/run_full_scale_v24.py
"""DEPRECATED thin shim — behaviour folded into scripts/run_full_scale.py.

Plan-V4 consolidation collapsed the byte-identical run_full_scale_v10..v29
launchers into a single parameterized launcher. This shim preserves the old
entrypoint (kept, not deleted, because archive handovers / preservation-test
skip messages reference this path) by delegating to:

    python scripts/run_full_scale.py --variant v24

Any extra CLI args are forwarded verbatim, so
    python scripts/run_full_scale_v24.py --out-root outputs/custom
still works exactly as before.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import sys
from pathlib import Path

_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
if str(_REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO_ROOT))


def main() -> int:
    from scripts.run_full_scale import run

    return run("v24", sys.argv[1:])


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

=== consolidated launcher head ===
180 scripts/run_full_scale.py
"""Consolidated full-scale launcher — one parameterized entrypoint for the
V10..V29 sweep launchers (Plan-V4 consolidation).

Previously each sweep iteration shipped its own ``run_full_scale_vNN.py``
wrapper. Those wrappers were byte-for-byte identical except for:
  * a per-variant env-capacity profile (``_VNN_ENV`` dict), and
  * the default ``--out-root`` (``outputs/full_scale_vNN``).

Every wrapper's ``main()`` did the same three things: apply its env profile
(non-destructively — never overriding a value already set in the parent shell
or ``.env``), default ``--only clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm`` /
``--out-root outputs/full_scale_vNN`` when absent, then delegate to
``scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3.main``.

This module reproduces each historical variant's exact behaviour behind a
``--variant`` flag, so ``run_full_scale.py --variant v27`` is equivalent to the
old ``run_full_scale_v27.py``. The thin ``run_full_scale_vNN.py`` shims now call
straight through to this launcher.

Usage:
    python scripts/run_full_scale.py --variant v29
    python scripts/run_full_scale.py --variant v27 --out-root outputs/my_run
    python scripts/run_full_scale.py --variant v25 --only clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm

Any extra CLI args (``--only``, ``--out-root``, ...) are forwarded verbatim to
run_honest_sweep_r3, exactly as the old wrappers forwarded ``sys.argv``.

NOTE: run_full_scale_v30_phase2.py is intentionally NOT folded in here — it is a
live by-path dependency of src/polaris_graph/audit_ir/v30_sweep_job_runner.py and
has distinct phase-2 behaviour, so it remains a standalone script.

content.content:
=== VARIANT_ENV table in launcher ===
_BASE_ENV: dict[str, str] = {
    # Sweep-level retrieval knobs (scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py)
    "PG_SWEEP_MAX_SERPER": "50",
    "PG_SWEEP_MAX_S2": "50",
    "PG_SWEEP_FETCH_CAP": "500",
    # Generator evidence pool cap
    "PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN": "600",
    # Budget cap
    "PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN": "10.00",
    # M-23 access-bypass feature flags
    "PG_UNPAYWALL_ENABLED": "1",
    "PG_CRAWL4AI_ENABLED": "1",
    "PG_FIRECRAWL_ENABLED": "0",   # per user directive: costs money
    "PG_TRAFILATURA_ENABLED": "1",
    # Scraper circuit breakers
    "PG_CRAWL4AI_TIMEOUT": "30",
    "PG_CIRCUIT_BREAKER_THRESHOLD": "8",
    "PG_CIRCUIT_BREAKER_COOLDOWN": "120",
    # Sci-Hub DISABLED (legal/provenance, I-faith-002); CORE is the OA source
    "PG_SCIHUB_ENABLED": "0",
}


def _profile(**overrides: str) -> dict[str, str]:
    env = dict(_BASE_ENV)
    env.update(overrides)
    return env


# Exact reproduction of each historical wrapper's env profile.
VARIANT_ENV: dict[str, dict[str, str]] = {
    "v10": _profile(),
    "v23": _profile(),
    "v24": _profile(),
    "v25": _profile(),
    "v26": _profile(
        PG_M41D_HC_QUOTA="2",
    ),
    "v27": _profile(
        PG_M41D_HC_QUOTA="2",
        PG_SWEEP_MAX_REGULATORY_ANCHORS="12",
    ),
    "v28": _profile(
        PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN="300",
        PG_M41D_HC_QUOTA="2",
        PG_SWEEP_MAX_REGULATORY_ANCHORS="12",
        PG_SWEEP_MAX_PRIMARY_TRIAL_ANCHORS="15",
    ),
    "v29": _profile(
        PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN="300",
        PG_M41D_HC_QUOTA="2",
        PG_SWEEP_MAX_REGULATORY_ANCHORS="12",
        PG_SWEEP_MAX_PRIMARY_TRIAL_ANCHORS="15",
    ),
}

DEFAULT_ONLY = "clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm"


def _apply_env(variant: str, env: dict[str, str]) -> None:
    """Export the variant env. Does NOT overwrite values already set by the
    user in the parent shell or loaded from .env (override=False semantics),
    matching every original run_full_scale_vNN wrapper.
    """
    tag = f"[{variant} env]"
    for key, val in env.items():
        existing = os.environ.get(key)
        if existing is None or existing == "":
            os.environ[key] = val
            print(f"{tag}  {key} = {val}")
        else:
            print(f"{tag}  {key} = {existing}  (already set, not overriding)")

input.content:
You are a strict release gate reviewing a mechanical file-rename + launcher-consolidation refactor in a large Python research repo. Your job is to decide whether the executed changes are reference-complete and behaviour-preserving. Answer only from the evidence supplied; do not assume unstated facts. End with exactly one verdict token: FILE-RENAME-SAFE or FILE-RENAME-REVISE (and if REVISE, name the specific broken reference).

## What was done
28 files were renamed and 8 near-duplicate launcher scripts were consolidated. A verifier ran `pytest --collect-only` and a byte-hash "oracle" harness before and after.

## Rename classification method
Each rename target was pre-classified as SAFE-STATIC (only normal `import`/`from ... import` references + docs, mechanically fixable) or DYNAMIC-SKIP (referenced via `spec_from_file_location` by-path load, subprocess string path, CLI arg, CI workflow path filter, or a filename-string test assertion). DYNAMIC-SKIP files were intentionally NOT renamed because a file rename would break an untracked path/CLI/CI surface.

## The 6 DYNAMIC-SKIP files (correctly held back, NOT renamed)
1. scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py — CI path filter + `python -m scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3` docker entrypoint + 4 by-path loaders.
2. scripts/iarch007_release_invariant_check.py — sibling canary builds the exact filename literal + spec_from_file_location; test _load_path_module.
3. scripts/iarch010_replay_breadth_faithfulness_harness.py — string path in a subprocess dispatch table.
4. scripts/pipeline_diced_preflight.py — by-path test loader + 2 subprocess consumers + --diced CLI arg.
5. scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py — hard-coded by-path invocation in a shell launcher.
6. scripts/run_full_scale_v30_phase2.py — by-path in src/polaris_graph/audit_ir/v30_sweep_job_runner.py:457 (`sweep_script=repo_root/'scripts'/'run_full_scale_v30_phase2.py'`) + filename assertion in tests/polaris_graph/test_honest_sweep_job_runner.py:336 (`sweep_script.name == 'run_full_scale_v30_phase2.py'`).

## Execute results
- files_renamed: 28
- importers_fixed: 52 (every normal import updated to the new module path)
- launcher_created: scripts/run_full_scale.py (parameterized `--variant vNN`)
- scripts_consolidated: 8 (v10,v23,v24,v25,v26,v27,v28,v29) — the old scripts are KEPT as 31-line thin call-through shims that do `from scripts.run_full_scale import run; return run("vNN", sys.argv[1:])`. They were kept (not deleted) because archive handovers / preservation-test pytest.skip() message strings reference those paths.
- collection_after: 16738 tests collected, 11 errors (identical to baseline 16738/11)

## Independent verification I (the gate operator) performed before consulting you
1. grep for stale imports of every OLD renamed module name (honest_pipeline, report_assembler_v2, synthesizer_v2, verifier_v2, state_v3, v30_contract_synthesizer, honest_sweep_integration, honest_sweep_job_runner, pathB_capture, pathB_runner, retrieve.lethal) across src/ scripts/ tests/ → ZERO stale import statements remain (only docstring prose mentions inside the renamed files themselves).
2. Oracle byte-hash identical before/after: 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98. Collection at baseline.
3. Launcher behaviour-preservation: I diffed the ORIGINAL git v29 env dict against the launcher's reproduced v29 profile. Byte-for-byte identical values (PG_SWEEP_MAX_SERPER=50, PG_SWEEP_MAX_S2=50, PG_SWEEP_FETCH_CAP=500, PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN=300, PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN=10.00, PG_UNPAYWALL_ENABLED=1, PG_CRAWL4AI_ENABLED=1, PG_FIRECRAWL_ENABLED=0, PG_TRAFILATURA_ENABLED=1, PG_CRAWL4AI_TIMEOUT=30, PG_CIRCUIT_BREAKER_THRESHOLD=8, PG_CIRCUIT_BREAKER_COOLDOWN=120, PG_SCIHUB_ENABLED=0, PG_M41D_HC_QUOTA=2, PG_SWEEP_MAX_REGULATORY_ANCHORS=12, PG_SWEEP_MAX_PRIMARY_TRIAL_ANCHORS=15). Env application uses override=False semantics (never overwrites a value already set in shell/.env) — matches every original wrapper. Default --only=clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm and --out-root=outputs/full_scale_vNN injected only when absent — matches originals. Extra CLI args forwarded verbatim to run_honest_sweep_r3.main.
4. v30_phase2 by-path dependency confirmed still intact (line 457 path construction + line 336 test assertion both still name the un-renamed file). The launcher docstring explicitly excludes v30_phase2 from consolidation.

## The ONE unresolved item: 2 dangling references
One SAFE rename MOVED scripts/_retired_2026_06_14/pg_compose_openai_validation.py to archive/2026_06_14_retired_scripts/pg_compose_openai_validation.py (no leading underscore, and the archive dir has NO __init__.py).

Two sibling retired scripts still import the OLD leading-underscore module path:
- scripts/_retired_2026_06_14/pg_geval_openai.py:29 → `from archive._2026_06_14_retired_scripts.pg_compose_openai_validation import OpenAIShimClient`
- scripts/_retired_2026_06_14/pg_compose_production_scale.py:45 → same import

Both would raise ModuleNotFoundError if executed (leading-underscore path `archive._2026_06_14_retired_scripts` does not exist; the real dir is `archive.2026_06_14_retired_scripts` which is also not an importable package since it has no __init__.py).

I verified: NOTHING imports these two broken scripts (grep across src/scripts/tests = zero external importers). They are not named test_*, so pytest does not collect them. They live under scripts/_retired_2026_06_14/ (a retired/dead-code directory). Hence collection (16738/11) and the oracle hash are unaffected — the breakage is confined to two already-dead leaf scripts that nothing runs.

## Questions to answer
1. Are all EXECUTED file renames reference-complete — every importer fixed, no dangling reference, no dynamic/string/CLI/CI reference to a RENAMED file left broken? (Distinguish: is the only dangling ref the 2 dead-leaf retired imports, which reference a MOVED-and-already-dead file, not any live surface?)
2. Is the launcher consolidation behaviour-preserving — old vNN scripts still work as call-throughs with no behavioural drift?
3. Were the 6 DYNAMIC-REF files correctly skipped?
4. Does the 2-dangling-ref item warrant REVISE, or is it cosmetic dead-code debt that is safe to land (given zero live-surface impact, baseline collection, byte-identical oracle)? State your reasoning explicitly.

End with FILE-RENAME-SAFE or FILE-RENAME-REVISE (+ the specific broken reference if REVISE).

4. REVISE is warranted. “Reference-complete” is stricter than “no live-surface regression.” Dead-code status lowers impact but does not erase two demonstrably dangling references caused by an executed move.
